title: Heart Curve Manim
prompt: Run `uv init` before you start. Build a Python Manim scene for `(x^2 + y^2 - 1)^3 = x^2 y^3`. Display the exact model name `{{MODEL_NAME}}` in the scene. Keep the equation, axes, and curve centered, non-overlapping, and fully visible in frame. The root `.run` script must render the scene and exit non-zero on failure.

title: Notion To Airtable-Style Table
prompt: Create a local Airtable-style web app that imports `fixtures/notion-export.json`, infers sensible columns, normalizes inconsistent values, preserves every source row, and clearly shows both the source record and the cleaned record. Include an audit view that explains how each normalized field was derived.

title: Founder Match
prompt: Create a Tinder-like app for SF founders with swipe left/right interactions, founder profile cards, a match view, and realistic local mock data. Make it mobile-first, visually polished, and clearly usable on small screens without broken layouts.

title: Habit Roaster
prompt: Build a habit tracker that roasts the user when they miss habits. On a failed streak, generate a sad or stylized version of the uploaded profile picture locally and pair it with a short AI-style roast joke that is funny but not abusive. Keep the whole flow self-contained and offline-friendly.

title: Pokemon Guessing Game
prompt: Recreate a Gen 1 Pokemon Guessing Game from this reference description: dark retro pixel style, centered title ('Pokemon Guessing Game'), top control row with input, Hint button, mute icon button, Reset button, trophy button, and score box; green feedback text ('Correct!'); and a large grid of numbered square tiles where correct guesses reveal Pokemon sprites in the right slots (for example #1 Bulbasaur, #4 Charmander, #7 Squirtle). Match layout, spacing, and retro styling closely.

title: Mock Media CLI
prompt: Create a Bun CLI using `https://github.com/sst/opentui` with two commands: `image` and `video`. Instead of calling live APIs, read presets and mock provider behavior from `fixtures/mock-media-provider.json`, validate arguments, show progress and status output, and write generated artifact metadata into `output/`. The root `.run` script should execute a smoke path that demonstrates both commands.

title: Interactive 3D Pumpkin
prompt: Create an interactive 3D pumpkin using Three.js and GSAP with smooth rotation or parallax on pointer movement and a short intro animation on load. The finished page should feel intentional, responsive, and visually polished on desktop and mobile browser sizes.

title: Developer Link Hub
prompt: Build a Linktree clone for developers in a neo-brutalist, minimal, GitHub-inspired design. Use `fixtures/dev-profile.json` and `fixtures/github-activity.json` as the only data sources. Include the concrete profile name `Frances O.`, developer links, and a GitHub commits dashboard in the same style. Add a GSAP loading animation where a GitHub logo fills in, plus a subtle ASCII background animation that fits the aesthetic.

title: Weekly Summary Task
prompt: Read the bundled commit activity in `fixtures/weekly-git-activity.json` and generate a local Linear-style task file at `output/weekly-summary-task.json`. The task title must be exactly `Weekly Summary`, and the description must contain exactly 5 bullet points that explain the most important changes in simple language.

title: Zodiac Gallery Mobile
prompt: Create a mobile astrology app with Expo that uses `fixtures/zodiac-gallery.json` and `fixtures/nano-banana-notes.md` to drive a self-contained mock image generation flow for each zodiac sign. Include sign selection, generate action, loading state, and a simple history or gallery view.

title: Vision File Renamer
prompt: Create a local-first tool that uses a pluggable vision adapter with bundled mock data from `fixtures/vision-descriptions.json` to analyze the files in `fixtures/vision-batch/` and rename them with accurate, human-readable, collision-safe filenames. Support batch folders and dry-run mode. The root `.run` script should demonstrate a dry run and a real rename against a copied sample directory.

title: Steampunk Pomodoro Mobile
prompt: Create a mobile Pomodoro timer app in steampunk style with Expo or React Native. As the user completes focus sessions, a visible `compute power` meter should grow and unlock tasteful visual flourishes. Keep the scope self-contained with local state and mock data only.

title: Playable Ad Game
prompt: Reproduce a game concept commonly seen in low-quality mobile ads, but make it genuinely playable and fun. Deliver the build plan as 12 concise prompts or steps, each adding one meaningful feature or polish improvement, and ship the final playable web build in the same workspace.

title: Reference Post Rebuild
prompt: Rebuild the experience described in `fixtures/reference-post.md`. Match the core UX flow, layout, motion, and visual style as closely as possible using only the local reference notes and placeholder assets that you create inside the assigned folder.
